lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek a shield that will protect me from dragonbreath.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: A knight going on a dragon quest, hmm? What dragon do you intend to slay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elvarg, the dragon of Crandor island!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Elvarg? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Well, you're a braver [man/woman] than I!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is everyone so scared of this dragon?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Back in my father's day, Crandor was an important city-state. Politically it was as important as Falador and Varrock and its ships traded with every port.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: But, one day, when I was little, all contact was lost. The trading ships and the diplomatic envoys just stopped coming.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I remember my father being very scared. He posted lookouts on the roof to warn if the dragon was approaching. All the city rulers worried that Elvarg would devastate the whole continent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better leave that dragon alone.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: That's a relief. I would hate to see such a promising adventurer cut down in [his/her] prime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you going to give me the shield or not?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: If you really think you're up to it then perhaps you are the one who can kill this dragon.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Take care out there. If you kill it...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: If you kill it, for Saradomin's sake make sure it's really dead!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Very wise. There are some monsters that are best left alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no dragon in particular. I just feel like killing a dragon.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I don't have an infinite supply of these shields, you know. I'll only give one for a truly worthy cause.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no dragon in particular. I just feel like killing a dragon.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Of course. Now you've slain Elvarg, you've earned the right to call the shield your own!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any quests for me?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Well, it's not really a quest but I recently discovered this strange talisman.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: It seems to be mystical and I have never seen anything like it before. Would you take it to the head wizard at

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: the Wizards' Tower for me? It's just south-west of here and should not take you very long at all. I would be awfully grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, no problem.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Thank you very much, stranger. I am sure the head wizard will reward you for such an interesting find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: As you wish, stranger, although I have this strange feeling that it is important. Unfortunately, I cannot leave my castle unattended.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: The only task remotely approaching a quest is the delivery of that talisman I gave you to the head wizard of the Wizards' Tower,

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: south-west of here. I suggest you deliver it to him as soon as possible. I have the oddest feeling that it is important...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Did you speak to the head wizard for me yet, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I lost that air talisman that you gave me.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Ah, that would explain it. One of my servants found this outside, and it seemed too much of a coincidence that more than one strange

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: object would appear on my land in such a short period of time. Please take this to the head wizard at the Wizards' Tower, south-west of here, and don't lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: The only job I had was the delivery of that talisman, so I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: No, all is well for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find money?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I've heard that the blacksmiths are prosperous amongst the peasantry. Maybe you could try your hand at that?
